MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Amended Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Claude Wyle (cwyle@ccwlawyers.com) for Plaintiff, Michelle Sayada.
Jim Weixel (415-949-1911; jvw@darlaw.com) of DEMLER, ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP for Defendant, Venkateswara Kamireddy.
Sheena Patel for Defendant, Hertz Vehicles LLC.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VENKATESWARA KAMIREDDY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Amended Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues is denied. Summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (defendant's negligence). The court exercises its discretion per Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(e) and denies the motion because Kamireddy's assertion that he revoked Kativarapu's permission to use the vehicle is a material fact to which he is the sole witness since Kativarapu is in India and unavailable. (See Weixel Dec., Ex. A [Kamireddy Depo., 58:1-60:3].) In addition, there exists a triable issue of material fact whether defendant expressly or impliedly permitted Kativarapu to use the vehicle that was involved in the accident. Plaintiff presents evidence that a reasonable trier of fact could rely upon to doubt Kamireddy's testimony. (Plaintiff's Additional Facts, 1-44.) The court denies summary adjudication as to issue 2 (limitation of liability). The statutory limitation under California Vehicle Code section 17151(a) "does not apply . . . to a vehicle owner's own common law negligence, as distinguished from the owner's statutory vicarious liability for the operator's negligence." (Fremont Compensation Insurance Co. v. Hartnett (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 669, 675-676.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
